content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcar PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 95914-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
December 13,2005
Gregg Payne
Arts Commission
City of Chico
411 Main Street
P.O. Box 3420
Chico, CA95927
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. l-05-228
Dear Mr. Payne:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the political Reform Act (the ,,Act,,).
As your request seeks general assistance and does not provide specific
information about a particular governmental decision, we are treating yirrr r"q.r.st as one
for informal assistance.r This letter is solely based on the facts prese]ied to us in your
request. The Commission does not act as a finder of fact when issuing advice or informal
assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FppC Ops. 71.)
QUESTION
May you complete the Bidwell Park Pillar project ("Pillar project,), which was
approved for completion and funded through the Chico City Bidwell irark-& playground
Commission in June 2004?
CONCLUSION
The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not restictor prohibit any
specificprivate conducl. Thus, the Act would not prohibit you from completing ihe
Pillar Project. However, please note that other laws may apply as discussed beiow.
t Government Code sections 8 I 000 - glo14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
L8109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
fomral written advice. (Regulation 18329(c), copy enilosed.)
;i'f.i


File No. I-05-228
Page No. 2
FACTS
You were appointed to the City of Chico's Arts Commission in March 2005.
Three years prior to your appointment, on April 29,2002,the city,s park and
Playground Commission ("Park Commission') approved yourproposal 
-for 
the pillar
Project, which involves painting artwork on the piitarr in biAwel Fark under the
Highway 99 overpass.
You began work on the Pillar Project in2002. However, due to numerous
funding and permit problems, only two of the twentypillars have been completed. On
December 17,2004 permits for the remainder of the project were approved by Caltrans.
The Arts Commission has never been involved in the Pillar project. The project
has evolved solely through collaboration between you, the Park Commission andthe city
council.
You would like to know if you may complete the project that you started in2002.
ANALYSIS
Conflict-of-Interest Prohibition: Section 87100 prohibits anypublic official
from making, participating in making, or otherwise using hir or her oticial position to
influence a goverlmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
A public official has a "financial interest" in agovernmental decision within the
meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the govemmental decision will
have a material financial effect on one or more of the public official's economic interests.
(Section 87103; regulation 1S700(a).) The Commission has adopted a standard eight-step
analysis for deciding whether an individual has a disqualifting conflict of interest in a
grven govemmental decision. (Regulation 1 8700(bX 1 )-(g).)
1. Are you considered a o.public officialrr?
The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act pertain only to public officials. A
public official includes "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
g9v9ry1nent agency . . . )' (Seclion 82048; regulation 1g701(a)(1).) As a member of the
city's Arts Commission, you are a public official subject to the Acf's conflict-of-interest
provisions.
2. will you be making, participating in making, or influencing a
goYernmental decision?
A public official "makes a governmental decision" when the official , actrng
within the authority of his or her office or position, votes on a matter, obligates or
commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contricfual
agreement on behalf of his or her agency. (Regulati on 78702.L ) In addition to actually


File No. I-05-228
Page No. 3
voting on these matters, if a public official engages in negotiations without significant
substantive review, provides advice or makes recommenJations, he or she wiit Ue
"participating" in a decision (Regulation 18702.2). A public official also ,,influences,, a
decision if he or she appears before or otherwise attempts to influenc e anymember,
officer, employee or consultant of an agency. (Regulatlon 1[,702.3.)
The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not restictor prohibit any
specifrc private conduct--rather they regulate official conduct, where tiris conduci^uy
have a foreseeable and materi al financial effect on any of your economic interests. There
is no such decision before you. Thus, the Act would hot piohibit you from completing
the Pillar Project.
However, there may be other applicable laws. You may wish to contact the
Attomey General's Office with respect to possible application of Govemment Code
section 1090, which generally provides that anofficei or employee of any governmental
agency may not make a contract in which the officer or employee has a finincial interest.
Moreover, your agency may also have a list of incompatible activities thatmay
restrict your private activities. The Commission does not have jurisdiction over either of
these potential restrictions and is not permitted to offer advice on bodies of law outside
the confines of the Act.
We therefore strongly recommend that you consult the Attorney General,s office
regarding the possible application of section 1090 andlor your city attorney regarding
other possible confl icts.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
i
Enclosure
